title: Topological isotopy with endpoint $K_0=K$ at $t=0$ asserted, not proven

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body: A *topological isotopy* on $[0,1]$ is a continuous family of **embeddings** $\phi_t:M\to\mathbb R^4$. The Theorem asserts $\Lambda_{df_i}\to K$ in Hausdorff distance and that interpolation+reparametrisation yields a topological isotopy on $[0,1]$ with $K_0=K$. But $C^1$-smallness of $f_i$ only gives $C^0$/Hausdorff closeness of *images*; it does not produce a continuous family of embeddings up to and including $t=0$ converging to a homeomorphism onto the singular set $K$. The endpoint claim (injectivity/continuity at $t=0$) needs explicit uniform control: construct $\phi_t$ with $\phi_t\to\phi_0$ in $C^0$ (image $K$), uniformly injective including $t=0$.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
**Fix for the endpoint claim ($K_0=K$ at $t=0$ as a genuine topological isotopy).**

The original Section-5 assembly only argued Hausdorff convergence of *images* $\Lambda_{df_i}\to K$ and asserted that interpolation+reparametrization yields a topological isotopy on $[0,1]$ with $K_0=K$. As the critic noted, Hausdorff closeness of images is too weak: it neither produces a continuous family of *embeddings* up to and including $t=0$, nor controls injectivity at the limit. I rewrote the assembly (now Sections 5–6) to construct the embeddings explicitly and prove continuity+injectivity on the closed interval.

**Key idea: parametrize by the fixed base $K$ via the conormal fibration.**
The fibration $\{L_z\}$ has disjoint fibres covering $\nu K$, giving a projection $\pi:\nu K\to K$. I introduce *sections* of $\pi$ (continuous $s:K\to\nu K$ with $\pi\circ s=\mathrm{id}_K$). New Lemma (section-embed): every section is a topological embedding of $K$, and — crucially — injectivity is the one-line tautology $\pi\circ s=\mathrm{id}_K$ ⇒ $z=z'$, valid for *every* section with **no smallness hypothesis**, hence at the singular base too (the zero section, image exactly $K$). Compactness of $K$ + Hausdorffness of $\mathbb R^4$ upgrade continuous injection to embedding.

**Quantitative endpoint control.** New Lemma (section-small): $\|s_f\|_{C^0}\le C\,\|df\|_{C^0}$ with $C$ a finite-atlas constant, so sections $\to$ zero section uniformly as $\|df\|_{C^0}\to0$.

**The path must stay in $\mathscr S(K)$ — I do NOT scale.** I initially tried scaling the section ($\sigma_t=t\sigma$), but that is *wrong*: $\mathscr S(K)$ is the affine coset $g+C^\infty(K)$ with $0\notin\mathscr S(K)$, so $t g\notin\mathscr S(K)$ and the intermediate "Lagrangians" would not be smooth/graphical. Instead I use the *explicit* smoothing family $f^\varepsilon=\sum_\alpha\rho_\alpha\sum_\sigma\chi_\sigma^\varepsilon f_\sigma$ already built in Lemma~\ref{lem:small}/\ref{lem:small-global}. New Lemma (cont-family) records that this family (a) is jointly smooth in $(\varepsilon,z)$ on $(0,1]\times K$, (b) has $\|df^\varepsilon\|_{C^0}\le M\varepsilon$, (c) lies below the graphicality threshold for small $\varepsilon$ — and every member is genuinely in $\mathscr S(K)$.

**The isotopy.** Set $\phi_t:=s_{f^{\varepsilon(t)}}$ for $t\in(0,1]$, $\phi_0:=$ zero section $=\mathrm{id}_K$ (image $K$). Then:
- each $\phi_t$ is an embedding, injective uniformly including $t=0$ (section-embed);
- $t\mapsto\phi_t$ is continuous on $(0,1]$: $\rho(\phi_t,\phi_{t'})\le C\|df_t-df_{t'}\|_{C^0}$ with $t\mapsto df_t$ continuous by (a);
- continuity at $t=0$: $\rho(\phi_t,\phi_0)=\|s_{f_t}\|_{C^0}\le CM\,\varepsilon(t)\to0$.
Hence $t\mapsto\phi_t$ is continuous on the **closed** $[0,1]$ into embeddings of $K$, with $\phi_0=\mathrm{id}_K$ — a topological isotopy with endpoint the inclusion of $K$, exactly as required. The Hamiltonian property on $(0,1]$ comes from the smooth path of generating functions $f_t+q_K$ (smooth time-derivative $\partial_t f_t$) via the standard generating-function/Hamiltonian-isotopy correspondence.

**Net effect:** the endpoint continuity and injectivity (the gap) are now proven with explicit uniform control $\rho(\phi_t,\mathrm{id}_K)=O(\varepsilon(t))$, replacing the unjustified leap from Hausdorff convergence of images. I also corrected a genuine math error I caught while drafting (scaling out of $\mathscr S(K)$) and updated Remark 2 accordingly. Cross-references, theorem environments, and bibliography all check out; no LaTeX toolchain is installed here so I verified environment balance and label resolution programmatically.

Resolving.
